blocks.text: July 13th
The highly unpredictable adventure continues....

I woke up in the ravine beside the creek and I right away noticed I left the tent open. Of course there were about 20 mosquitoes flying around. Some how I only had about 10 new big bites and only on my legs and arms. I got lucky that there were only a few bites.
I then left the ravine and went into town to meet up with Egita.
We only had one goal today, that was see the naked man David. Yesterday there was a 3 hour line in the heat midday. So we went early.
I had left my bike with a guy in laundry mat and I gave him 5 euros to watch the bike for 4 hours.
Then we got to the art gallery where the the famous statue of David is, there was still a 3 hour wait in the line up....
That was terrible, but at least there was no heat. Everyone was complaining about the line up 😂
We then went inside after waiting forever in the line up and saw the famous statues.
Wow it's incredible. I have never seen statue like it before.
We checkout out the other art pieces and left after about 30 mins.
I went to take a nap - I found a spot in a plaza. Egita went to return the rented bike and check out of her hostel.
Egita and I decided we want to keep travelling together. Since she has already booked and paid for hostels in Genova, a different city, we had to go there next.
After my nap we met up at the train station.
I hate the trains in Europe. It's always complete chaos.
The first train we wanted to take was full, so we missed it.
The next train was delayed for 30 mins, then it was cancelled.
Finally we got a train but not direct. We had to go to Pisa first.
Pisa...
I always really wanted to see the leaning tower of pisa. And now fate had given me the opportunity.
We took the train, this time it came on time, and it was backed full and uncomfortable to ride on with the bike...

The train was packed full for over an hour shoulder to shoulder. It was so fully I couldn't move at all. And some the Italians were frustrated by the bike (they didn't know I understood what they were saying hehe).
We got to Pisa and had an hour before the next train so I made the decision to see all of Pisa by bike in 30 minutes.
I raced around the streets of Pisa and saw the leaning tower!
Wow it was really cool. It was a lighter color than I expected and also it was smaller than I expected. It was still really cool it see in person.
I biked back to the train station and we got the next train to Genova.
We arrived, I cooked us both pasta with the camping gear, and we parted ways for the night. Egita to her hostel, and me...
I had no idea where I was going.
I saw on the map a forest 2km away. It looked like it could be a potential camp spot. I thought that it would be easy to get to since it was so close.
I didn't realize it was up on the mountain....
It was 2km but 2km up and very steep. I was quickly covered in sweat again. Some of the steepest roads I have biked on.
I finally got to the top to find out that all the entrances to the forest were locked with a gate and had a massive wall.
Great....
Then I saw this broken part of the wall but it was a few meters up and I would have to lift my bike and climb a little to get there.
I saws guy who looked about my age nearby and asked him if he would help me lift up the bike up the wall.

He looked at me like I was crazy. He said he knew a spot much easier to camp and to follow him. I said all the gates up there I saw were closed and locked. Then he said he knows how to get inside and open them for me...
I followed him to the door, and I was like. Where am I going hahaha. I just went with it.
The gate said private property no trespassing. I figured I would go in and quickly go into the forest to stay out of site.
he came around to let me in but couldn't get the door open. Then he said to go around the back to another entrance.
I met him at the back and I had to carry my bike down some stairs but eventually we arrived.
It was in front of this massive building that looked like some type of housing.
I asked him where I was and he explained that his was a house for youth refugees. He said he was one of them.
He said I could just camp there. I did not want to camp right there in front of the building since it was so obvious and visible. I looked around the forest nearby for a spot more hidden from sight.
I had no luck finding a spot. Everything was too steep for camping with a tent.
I said screw it. I started to set up my tent in the spot he had showed me, if anyone asked I would just say I someone told me to camp there.
only about 1 minutes later a car pulled up and asked me what the hell I was doing there. That it was private property and he was the custodian of the land.
I told him in Italian everything that had happened and that someone told me to camp there and he was like...
No you can its dangerous here - there are the life's and wild boars etc.
We talked for a while and eventually we told me to come with him,

Then he offered me spare bedroom in the ground floor of his house. He lived above and uses the ground floor as storage, but he offered me the bed and let me use the shower and bathroom. I took a nice and much needed shower after that sweaty climb and slept amazing.
Reflection on the day.

items: Cancellation of trains allowed me to see Pisa. I was disappointed earlier in the trip when I realized Pisa was out of the way and I would get to see it. Fate brought me there.
Climbing a steep mountain, to a camp spot turns into a bed with a shower. How does this happen 😂

blocks.text: That's all for today. Enjoy the pictures :)

blocks.caption: Wall art outside the art museum
Me in the train - i had to hold my bike like this for 2 hours. Eventually the whole car was full of people shoulder to shoulder.
Making dinner in the park in Genova
Me climbing up at night for a place to sleep. Here was the view
The hole in the wall that goes to the forest
Where I waited for the guy to let me into the gated area
My bike in the guys house

blocks.text: That's all folks,
- Erik
